prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The European Union has introduced a comprehensive set of proposals, known as "Fit for 55," with the objective of decreasing its greenhouse gas emissions by 55% before the year 2030. These proposals were put forward by the European Commission during July of 2021. There is a possibility that these initiatives could be enacted into law during 2022, following an expedited legislative procedure. Among the provisions are enhanced backing for environmentally friendly transportation, renewable energy sources, and a new levy designated as the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism. This tariff will be applied to emissions associated with high-carbon goods imported from nations that have not implemented adequate greenhouse gas reduction strategies domestically. Furthermore, the plan suggests broadening the scope of the European Union Emissions Trading System to encompass the transport and heating sectors. When contrasted with the net-zero projection developed by the International Energy Agency, this particular plan incorporates a greater number of strategies aimed at guaranteeing that energy costs remain manageable. However, the environmental advocacy group Greenpeace has expressed disapproval of the package, contending that it is inadequate for effectively stopping global warming.
B. The European Union has proposed a package called Fit for 55, which aims to decrease the Union's greenhouse gas emissions by 55% by 2030. The proposal was proposed by the European Commission in July 2021 and may become law in 2022 through an expedited legislative process. The package includes additional support for clean transport, renewables, and a Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism on emissions for high-carbon imports from countries lacking sufficient greenhouse gas reduction measures. It also proposes to extend the European Union Emissions Trading System to transport and heat. Greenpeace has more measures to reduces the world to reduce
C. Fit for 55 is a package by the European Union designed to reduce the European Union's greenhouse gas emissions by 55% by 2030. The package was proposed in July 2021 by the European Commission. Under an accelerated legislative process, the plans may become law in 2022. Measures include additional support for clean transport, renewables, and a tariff called the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism on emissions for high-carbon imports from countries lacking sufficient greenhouse gas reduction measures of their own. It proposes to extend the European Union Emissions Trading System to transport and heat. Compared to the net-zero scenario from the International Energy Agency, the plan contains more measures to ensure that energy remains affordable. The environmental organization Greenpeace criticized the package for not being suitable for halting global
